commands.review_summary:
Adds a Telegram polling-session non-retryable classifier for reply-session initialization conflicts and a regression test that dead-letters the stale replay while draining the next same-topic update.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +43. Total +51 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main throws the reply-session initialization conflict, and unclassified spooled failures are released for retry. I did not run a live Telegram replay in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer delivery-policy acceptance are needed; there is no narrow mechanical repair for ClawSweeper to apply.

target.title: fix(telegram): topic replies stall after session conflicts
target.body:
## Summary

Dead-letter Telegram spooled replays that fail with a reply-session initialization conflict, so one stale replay cannot keep retrying forever and block later updates on the same lane.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users replying in a Telegram forum topic could see live sessions pause or appear stuck when an older spooled replay hit a reply-session initialization conflict. The failed replay was kept pending for retry, so it could re-enter the same permanent failure loop instead of letting later same-topic updates drain.

## Root Cause

Before this change, the Telegram spool replay path only treated missing agent harness failures and dispatch-dedupe rollback failures as non-retryable. Redacted live Gateway logs showed a different permanent replay shape: the replay failed with `reply session initialization conflicted for <redacted session route>` and then logged `spooled update <redacted id> failed; keeping for retry` repeatedly. Because that conflict did not match the non-retryable classifier, the spool retained the stale update and retried it instead of moving it aside.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Telegram polling session now classifies reply-session initialization conflicts as non-retryable spool failures alongside the existing permanent replay cases. That keeps the boundary in the spool replay owner, preserves normal retry behavior for transient failures, and avoids adding any session-store compatibility path or timeout-specific behavior.

## User Impact

Telegram forum-topic sessions can recover from this stale replay state automatically: the conflicting replay is moved to failed spool storage, and later updates in the topic can continue processing instead of sitting behind the same retry loop.

## Real Behavior Proof

Before: redacted live logs showed the same spooled Telegram replay repeatedly failing with a reply-session initialization conflict and being kept for retry.

After: the new regression test writes a stale same-topic replay followed by a later same-topic update. The stale replay is dead-lettered with `reply-session-init-conflict`, pending spool becomes empty, and the later same-topic update runs.

## Evidence

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "dead-letters (missing harness failures|reply session init conflicts|wrapped missing harness failures|grammY BotError-wrapped missing harness failures|buffered spooled claims when dispatch dedupe rollback fails)"

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       5 passed | 58 skipped (63)
```

```text
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts

All matched files use the correct format.
```

```text
git diff --check

(no output)
```

```text
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local

autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.83)
```

## Verification

Focused spool replay regression coverage passed after formatting. The validation intentionally stayed scoped to `extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts` and `extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` because the change is a narrow classifier addition in the existing Telegram spool failure resolver.

## What Was Not Tested

I did not run the full extension suite or a live Telegram replay against production credentials from this branch. The public PR also omits private Telegram links, chat IDs, message IDs, and raw session identifiers from the live proof.
